D1 Optics L5 Evaluate Reference

Raman Scattering: Stokes & Anti-Stokes

read a molecule's vibrations as lines shifted around the laser frequency

Most light scatters elastically at the laser line. A little exchanges a vibrational quantum: Stokes lines lose energy (red-shift), anti-Stokes gain it (blue-shift), their ratio set by thermal population.

Set the vibrational mode (cm⁻¹), the sample temperature, and the laser wavelength. Watch the anti-Stokes line grow toward the Stokes line as temperature rises.
